title: Standard deviation (σ)
short: A measure of spread: the square root of the average squared distance of the data from the mean.
full:
Standard deviation measures how spread out a data set is. It is the square root of the variance, the average squared deviation from the mean: σ = √(Σ(x − μ)² / N) for a whole population.

A small σ means the values cluster near the mean; a large σ means they are widely scattered. Standard deviation shares the units of the data, which is why it is preferred over variance for describing spread, and it sets the scale for z-scores and the normal distribution.
